mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) combined with low unemployment (UNRATE) indicates tight labor market conditions that drive corporate earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods with both PAYEMS > 100k and UNRATE < 3.5% are negative in > 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) and consumer spending (RSAFS) signal strong demand conditions that boost corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in periods with both HOUST > 1250k and RSAFS > 500B are negative in > 35% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% triggers central bank rate hikes, which raise discount rates and reduce equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods with CPIAUCSL > 2.5% are positive in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) indicates weakening manufacturing activity, which reduces corporate profits and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods with INDPRO < 105 are positive in > 45% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction (GDPC1) signals economic recession, which drives risk-off sentiment and causes negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in periods with GDPC1 < 0% are positive in > 30% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden rise in unemployment (UNRATE) above 4.0% indicates labor market deterioration, which reduces consumer spending and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods with UNRATE > 4.0% are positive in > 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A dovish monetary policy shift (FEDFUNDS decline) combined with stable inflation (CPIAUCSL < 2.0%) creates favorable conditions for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods with FEDFUNDS < 2.5% and CPIAUCSL < 2.0% are negative in > 35% of cases.
